tools.name: files_read
tools.description: Read the contents of a file.

path.description: File path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix)

tools.name: files_read_lines
tools.description: Read a range of lines from a file with line numbers. Returns lines in the format {line_number}\t{content}. Use this instead of files_read when you only need a specific portion of a file to reduce context usage, or when files_read output is truncated to read the remaining lines. When end_line is omitted, reads from start_line to the end of the file.

path.description: File path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix)

start_line.description: Line number to start reading at (1-indexed, inclusive)

end_line.description: Line number to end reading at (1-indexed, inclusive). When omitted, reads from start_line to the end of the file.

tools.name: files_list
tools.description: List files and directories at a given path. When an AGENTS.md file exists in the listed directory, its contents are automatically appended to the listing as a context section — this is not part of the ls output but an automatic context-loading feature.

path.description: Directory path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix)

long.description: Show detailed format with permissions, size, and dates

tools.name: files_search
tools.description: Search file contents for a pattern using grep. Returns matching lines with context.

path.description: File or directory path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix)

recursive.description: Search directories recursively. On by default — set to false only when searching a single file path.

files_with_matches.description: Show only file names that contain matches, not the matching lines

hintConditions.hint: file not found — use files_list to browse available files

execution.truncationHint: output truncated: {kept} of {total} lines shown; {omitted} more lines available. To continue reading, use files_read_lines with start_line: {next_start}; omit end_line to read the rest.

hintConditions.hint: file not found — use files_list to browse available files
use files_read_lines with these line numbers for surrounding context
